request.question: Cult of Personality is a song used by CM Punk as an entrance song for the WWE event held at which New Jersey stadium?

title: WrestleMania 29
Champion (Clement Marfo & The Frontline song)
Luke Gallows
WWE Slam City
King of the Ring (2010)
Elimination Chamber (2013)
Royal Rumble (2013)
Judgment of Princeton
Cult of Personality (song)
WrestleMania XXX
sentences: WrestleMania 29 (billed in print ads as WrestleMania NY/NJ) was the twenty-ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE, held on April 7, 2013 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
Nine professional wrestling matches were contested at the event, with one being shown on the pre-show.
"Champion" is a single release by English group Clement Marfo & The Frontline.
The song was released in the United Kingdom as a digital download on July 20, 2012.
The song peaked to number 38 on the UK Singles Chart.
The song features on the soundtrack of the video game "F1 2011" and the 2010 film "StreetDance 3D", and was the official theme song of the WWE Royal Rumble pay-per-view in 2013.
The song was also used CM Punk's 434 day reign as WWE Champion as a tribute
Andrew William Hankinson (born December 22, 1983) is an American professional wrestler and promoter, currently signed with WWE on the Raw brand under the ring name Luke Gallows.

During his first stint with the promotion, Hankinson also appeared briefly as an imposter of the wrestler Kane during 2006 and then returned as Festus, a seemingly mentally handicapped character who teamed with Jesse (Ray Gordy), but the team achieved little success.

After disappearing from television, Hankinson later became the enforcer and "disciple" of CM Punk, sporting a cleaner, militant look, with the Festus character's "real identity" revealed as Luke Gallows.
His first run in WWE ended in 2010.

He is perhaps best known for working for New Japan Pro Wrestling under the ring name Doc Gallows, and he also wrestled prominently under the TNA banner, where he was a member of the heel Aces & Eights stable under the ring name D.O.C. (Director of Chaos).

Along with Karl Anderson, Hankinson has won the WWE Raw Tag Team Championship once since returning to WWE, and he also held the IWGP Tag Team Championship three times with Anderson, making them one of only three tag teams (with The Steiner Brothers and The Dudley Boyz) to have held both titles between those promotions.
WWE Slam City was an animated show produced by WWE, which aired on the WWE Network and Wednesday evenings on Nicktoons.
WWE Slam City was based on the Mattel action figure series starring toyetic versions of the WWE roster circa 2013.

The series, filmed in the next generation of stop-motion animation, features a new WWE animated character The Finisher, who fires all of the WWE wrestlers and sends them to Slam City to find day jobs.
The wrestlers are plunged into new career challenges as they pack every street corner with work to do and scores to settle.

With John Cena as an auto mechanic, Rey Mysterio as a traffic guard, Alberto Del Rio as a coffee house barista, Mark Henry as a pizza shop mascot, CM Punk as an ice-cream man, Dolph Ziggler as an industry leader, Randy Orton as a zookeeper, Kane as a chef and Sheamus as a theater usher.
Daniel Bryan, The Miz, Big Show, Brock Lesnar, Santino Marella and Damien Sandow are still jobless but Santino and Sandow are the most desperate to find one.
WWE Legends Stone Cold and The Rock are also featured in Slam City.
The 'Stretch Move' feature of Slam City characters is highlighted in WWE Slam City.
The 2010 King of the Ring was a special edition of "Raw".
that aired on November 29, 2010, at the Wells Fargo Center in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
The program featured the nineteenth King of the Ring tournament and first since 2008.
Sheamus defeated John Morrison in the finals of the tournament to become King of the Ring.

Unlike the previous tournament that featured wrestlers from the "Raw," "SmackDown" and "ECW" brands, the 2010 version of the tournament only featured wrestlers from "Raw" and "SmackDown" (following the closure of the ECW brand in February 2010).

The last King of the Ring tournament which only featured wrestlers from both "Raw" and "SmackDown" was held in 2002 (following the WWE Brand Extension the previous month, but before the ECW brand was introduced as a third brand in 2006).

Despite the event being mainly focused on the King of the Ring concept, the main event featured Jerry Lawler (who was celebrating his birthday that day) facing the new WWE Champion The Miz for the WWE Championship in a Tables, Ladders and Chairs match which marked Lawler's first WWE Championship match.
Lawler lost due to distractions from Alex Riley and Michael Cole.
CM Punk was joined in commentary on the show.
Elimination Chamber (2013) (also known as No Escape (2013) in Germany) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on February 17, 2013, at the New Orleans Arena in New Orleans, Louisiana.
The pay-per-view was the fourth annual Elimination Chamber event.
This year's event was headlined by The Rock defending the WWE Championship against CM Punk.
Royal Rumble (2013) was the twenty-sixth annual Royal Rumble professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on January 27, 2013 at the US Airways Center in Phoenix, Arizona.
It was the first WWE pay-per-view of 2013, and featured John Cena winning the traditional Royal Rumble match.
In the main event, The Rock defeated CM Punk for the WWE Championship, recapturing the title for the first time since 2002, and ending Punk's long reign at 434 days.

The Judgment of Princeton was a wine tasting (or blind tasting) event held on 8 June 2012 during a conference of the American Association of Wine Economists held at Princeton University in Princeton, New Jersey.

The purpose of this event was to compare, by a blind tasting, of several French wines against wines produced in New Jersey in order to gauge the quality and development of the New Jersey wine industry.
Because New Jersey's wine industry is relatively young and small, it has received little attention in the world wine market.

The state's wine production has experienced growth in recent years largely as a result of state legislators offering new opportunities for winery licensing and repealing Prohibition-era laws that have constrained the industry's development in past years.

This event was modeled after a 1976 blind tasting event dubbed the "Judgment of Paris" in which French wines were compared to several wines produced in California when that state's wine industry was similarly young and developing.
The New Jersey wine industry heralded the results and asserted that the rating of New Jersey wines by the blind tasting's judges was a victory for the state's wine industry.
"Cult of Personality" is a song by rock band Living Colour.
It was their second single off their debut album, "Vivid", released on July 14, 1988.
"Cult of Personality" reached No. 13 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 9 on the "Billboard" Album Rock Tracks chart.
It also won the Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock Performance in 1990.
Its music video earned the MTV Video Music Award for Best Group Video and MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist.
The song was ranked No. 69 on VH1's "100 Greatest Hard Rock Songs".
The solo was ranked No. 87 in "Guitar World"' s "100 Greatest Guitar Solos" list.
It was also selected for inclusion in the musical reference book, "1001 Songs You Must Hear Before You Die: And 10,001 You Must Download".
The sections before the bridge are similar to a hook in Black Sabbath's "Wheels Of Confusion".
In 2007, the song was re-recorded and released for the video game "".
The re-recording later appeared in "Guitar Hero Smash Hits".
It also appeared in the video game "" on the radio station "Radio X".
The song is also the entrance music for former ROH and WWE wrestler turned UFC fighter, CM Punk, and Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.
The song also appeared on the soundtrack for the 2015 video game "NBA 2K16".
WrestleMania XXX (also written as WrestleMania 30) was the thirtieth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on April 6, 2014, at the Mercedes-Benz Superdome in New Orleans, Louisiana.
It was the first WWE event simultaneously broadcast live on pay-per-view television and WWE's new streaming media service, the WWE Network.
Seven professional wrestling matches were scheduled on the event's card and one pre-show match was streamed on the WWE Network.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Agent role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

Pipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.

Native output contract: The module must consume `question` and `summary_1` and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field named `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include every distinct entity token visible in the inputs in the single terse query and place at most one neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the targeted relation or attribute (for example: "EntityA EntityB director").
- If a visible disambiguator or attribute token appears adjacent to an entity in the inputs (for example a multiword service name, an explicit page title, a year, or an attribute marker like a birth/producer/service/series cue), include that disambiguator token together with the associated entity in the query rather than dropping or truncating it.
- If `summary_1` contains an explicit page-title token sequence (for example an instruction-like ExactPageTitle or Fetch page title), prefer that exact page-title token sequence as the query value.
- When the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single text field only):
- Output exactly one concise plain-text summary string (no extra fields, labels, markup, or metadata). Keep the summary to 1–2 short sentences unless the evidence forces a single-token identification.
- Primary goal: explicitly integrate the first-hop identity in `context` with facts found in `passages` so the final answer agent has the supported linkage it needs.

Explicit bridging requirement:
- When the passages supply a clear linking fact between the context entity and information in the passages, produce a concise 1–2 sentence integrated summary that: (1) names the context entity using the exact form present in `context`, (2) names the supporting entity or attribute using the exact form present in `passages`, and (3) states the explicit relation (membership, creator attribution, parentage, partnership, award, representation, founding, etc.) exactly as supported by the inputs. Phrase this as one short bridging sentence when possible.
- For relations that naturally involve two distinct facts (for example, parentage plus representation, or a person linked to a show and also to an occupation), it is acceptable to use two short sentences within the same single-string summary: e.g., one sentence for parentage and one for representation. Still observe the 1–2 sentence limit.

Inclusion of passage-supplied disambiguators (only when directly relevant):
- If a passage supplies canonical multi-token names or other disambiguating attributes needed to resolve the question, include them inline using the wording from the passages: append birthdates in parentheses immediately after the associated full canonical name (e.g., Name (YYYY–) ), state group type and formation year using the passage's phrasing (e.g., "a [group type] formed in [year]"), and include a canonical member list exactly as shown (comma-separated) only when the passage presents a definitive membership list. Do not add ancillary attributes that are not necessary to the identification or explicit linking required by the question.

Identification requests:
- If the question explicitly asks for a single identification (e.g., "which X" or otherwise requests a named token or label), return exactly the canonical name(s) as presented in the passages, comma-separated if multiple. Do not add explanatory clauses, relations, or extra text in these cases—only the canonical name(s).

When evidence is missing or ambiguous:
- If the passages do not supply a clear bridging fact, state that succinctly (for example: "No supporting evidence in the provided passages" or "Unclear from the provided sources"). Preserve uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Conciseness and fidelity constraints:
- Keep the summary short and factual (1–2 short sentences). Do not expand into biographies, exhaustive lists, or unrelated details.
- Use only facts and phrasing present in `context` and `passages`; do not introduce or infer unsupported information.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passage text, but short verbatim names or phrases necessary for fidelity are allowed.
- Treat the `context` field as the primary first-hop identity to be linked to `passages` when integrating evidence.
- Do not assert that a passage's member list is complete unless the passage explicitly states it is definitive.

Format enforcement:
- Return a single plain-text string only. Do not emit JSON, dictionaries, labels, bullets, or any structured artifact—only the concise natural-language summary string.

Produce the `summary` now based only on the supplied `question`, `context`, and `passages`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use summary_1 and summary_2 as the sole evidential basis for the final answer; give greatest weight to summary_2 (the later, post-retrieval integrated evidence). Do not introduce facts from outside these summaries.

Output rules (apply in order):

1) If summary_1 and summary_2 clearly and unambiguously agree on the same concise answer, set the value of `answer` to exactly one minimal string and nothing else. Before emitting that string, you may apply only the narrowly permitted normalizations listed below. After applying permitted normalizations, the emitted string must equal the minimal label text from the prioritized summary verbatim (see normalization constraints). Specific allowed single-token answers: for a binary yes/no conclusion, emit the single lowercase token: yes or no. For any other concise label, emit that label exactly as it appears in the summaries (trim leading/trailing whitespace and strip any surrounding punctuation or wrapper syntax such as surrounding parentheses, quotes, or JSON-like braces), subject only to the permitted normalizations. Do not add words, sentences, punctuation, quotes, brackets, or any other surrounding text.

2) Preserve multi-token labels: if any visible summary contains a multi-token form of the answer (for example a full personal name, a multi-word company or place), output that full multi-token form verbatim. Only shorten a label if a permitted normalization applies (see below).

3) Company/developer preference: when the question requests a company or developer and the summaries show a subordinate studio together with an explicit parent/publisher label (e.g., in parentheses or with phrasing like "published by"), choose the parent/publisher name as the single minimal answer string. If no parent/publisher is explicitly indicated, choose the best-supported developer/studio name from the summaries.

4) If the summaries conflict or are ambiguous, do NOT apply the unanimous-agreement rule in (1). Instead, use summary_2 as the primary basis and emit the minimal concise string supported by summary_2, applying rules (2) and (3) and the permitted normalizations as relevant. If summary_2 provides no concise supported answer, set `answer` to the minimal explicit token "unknown".

Permitted normalizations and extraction rules (apply only when the token(s) to emit appear verbatim in the prioritized summary and only to produce a strictly smaller, but still verbatim-supported, minimal label):
- Parenthetical/core-name stripping: if the candidate label in the prioritized summary includes common corporate suffixes or parenthetical abbreviations (for example variants like "Inc.", "LLC", or parenthetical acronyms), strip those suffixes/parentheticals and emit the core name only (do not invent or substitute other words).
- Hyphenated composite phrase extraction: if the prioritized summary presents a composite genre or label in the form "<modifier> X-Y" (for example an extra leading modifier followed by a hyphenated core like "classic action-adventure"), emit only the hyphenated core token exactly as shown ("X-Y").
- Trailing generic-descriptor removal: if the candidate minimal answer appears as a proper-name followed by a single trailing generic descriptor token of the form "<ProperName> crisis" (or an equivalent single-word generic classifier), strip the trailing generic token and emit only the proper-name sequence.
- Denomination-occupation reduction: if the question's scope requests a denomination/kind and the prioritized summary contains the form "<denomination> priest" (or equivalent denomination + occupational noun), emit only the denomination token(s) (i.e., the minimal denomination label).

Normalization constraints:
- Do not apply normalizations unless the text to emit appears verbatim in the prioritized summary (summary_2, or summary_1 only if summary_2 is silent for the answer).
- Normalizations may only remove or trim tokens; they must not add, invent, or substitute new content.
- Do not globally strip generic classifier nouns from multi-token geographic names or other proper nouns unless covered by the permitted rules above.

Tie-break and variant-selection rules:
- If a role-descriptor and a proper name co-occur in the prioritized summary in the form "<role> <Name>", prefer emitting the named individual exactly as shown (emit the Name only) unless the prioritized summary presents the role as the requested concise label explicitly without a name.
- For title or label-variant conflicts, prefer an alternate or parenthetical variant only when the prioritized summary explicitly marks that variant as an alias, alternate name, or the original-language title (or otherwise explicitly labels it as a variant) AND that variant matches the question's scope. If no such explicit labeling appears, emit the primary label supported by summary_2.

Strict emission rule (verbatim-emission):
- The final `answer` must equal the minimal label text from the prioritized summary verbatim after applying only the permitted normalizations above. Do not append or prepend occupational nouns, role descriptors, location qualifiers, or explanatory tokens that are not part of that verbatim label.

Formatting constraints (must always be followed):
- Produce exactly one field: `answer`.
- The value of `answer` must be a single minimal string (no surrounding text, punctuation, explanation, or structural wrappers).
- Do not output extra fields, labels, or markup.

Additional guidance:
- If summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge but summary_2 provides a resolved, supported conclusion, use the conclusion from summary_2.
- If a concise answer string can be drawn verbatim from the summaries, prefer that verbatim text over paraphrase.

End.

"Cult of Personality" reached No. 13 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 9 on the "Billboard" Album Rock Tracks chart.  It also won the Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock Performance in 1990. It was also selected for inclusion in the musical reference book, "1001 Songs You Must Hear Before You Die: And 10,001 You Must Download".  The sections before the bridge are similar to a hook in Black Sabbath's "Wheels Of Confusion".  In 2007, the song was re-recorded and released for the video game "".  The re-recording later appeared in "Guitar Hero Smash Hits".  It also appeared in the video game "" on the radio station "Radio X".  The song is also the entrance music for former ROH and WWE wrestler turned UFC fighter, CM Punk, and Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.  The song also appeared on the soundtrack for the 2015 video game "NBA 2K16".

WWE: Uncaged | WWE: Uncaged is a compilation album of unreleased professional wrestling entrance theme songs which was released by WWE on December 16, 2016 on online music stores.  The album features multiple tracks that were not available to the general public before the release of the album, most notably the first version of Chris Jericho's theme song "Break the Walls Down", which was composed by Jim Johnston and performed by Adam Morenoff used for his WWE debut back in 1999.  The album also features the acoustic versions of Batista's theme song, "I Walk Alone" and Randy Orton's theme song, "Voices" which were used for their WrestleMania 30 video packages, which aired on WWE programming before WrestleMania to get fans hyped and excited for their matches at the event.

Champion (Clement Marfo & The Frontline song) | "Champion" is a single release by English group Clement Marfo & The Frontline.  The song was released in the United Kingdom as a digital download on July 20, 2012.  The song peaked to number 38 on the UK Singles Chart.  The song features on the soundtrack of the video game "F1 2011" and the 2010 film "StreetDance 3D", and was the official theme song of the WWE Royal Rumble pay-per-view in 2013.  The song was also used CM Punk's 434 day reign as WWE Champion as a tribute

WWE No Mercy | No Mercy is an annual professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  The first No Mercy was held on May 16, 1999 in Manchester, England.  The event was held annually in October from 1999 to 2008, and from 2003 to 2006 it was exclusive to the SmackDown!  brand.  In 2016, the event returned as a "SmackDown" exclusive following the reinstated brand extension and aired on the WWE Network for the first time.  However in 2017, the pay-per-view will be a Raw branded event.  The song "No Mercy" by Jim Johnston was used as a regular theme song for the events in 2002, 2004, 2006, and 2007.  In the 2016 and 2017 events, the theme song was "No Mercy" by KIT.

Line in the Sand (Motörhead song) | Line in the Sand is a 2004 rock song from WWE's soundtrack album, "".  The song used to be the entrance theme of the professional wrestling alliance, Evolution.  The song is performed by Motörhead.

WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour | The WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour was a professional wrestling televised event held by World Wrestling Entertainment and which took place at the Westpac Stadium in Wellington, New Zealand on March 4, 2006.  The event was taped and later aired on WWE 24/7 in the United States.

Stadium anthem | Stadium anthems or sports anthems or arena anthems are a musical genre identifying songs that are played over the public address systems at stadiums and arenas during breaks in the action to rally the fans.  Unlike college fight songs, most stadium anthems were not written primarily for use at sports events, though compilations such as "ESPN Presents Stadium Anthems" and the ""Jock"" series occasionally feature remixed versions of these songs designed to segue together or to accentuate the rhythm or other elements of the songs.  Some football events have their own anthems, but they are not played during breaks, but they are played as entrance music.  The most notable anthems are the FIFA Anthem and the UEFA Champions League Anthem.

hop2_docs: WrestleMania 29 | WrestleMania 29 (billed in print ads as WrestleMania NY/NJ) was the twenty-ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE, held on April 7, 2013 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.  Nine professional wrestling matches were contested at the event, with one being shown on the pre-show.

WrestleMania 31 | WrestleMania 31 was the thirty-first annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  It took place on March 29, 2015, at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California.

MetLife Stadium | MetLife Stadium is an American sports stadium located in East Rutherford, New Jersey.  It is part of the Meadowlands Sports Complex and serves as the home stadium for two National Football League (NFL) franchises: the New York Giants and the New York Jets.  The stadium is owned by the MetLife Stadium Company, a joint venture of the Giants and Jets, who jointly built the stadium using private funds on land owned by the New Jersey Sports and Exposition Authority.  The stadium opened as New Meadowlands Stadium in 2010.  In 2011, MetLife, an insurance company based in New York City, acquired the naming rights to the stadium.  At a construction cost of approximately $1.6 billion, it is the most expensive stadium ever built and is the second-largest stadium in the NFL in terms of seating capacity.

WrestleMania XXVI | WrestleMania XXVI was the twenty-sixth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE), which took place on March 28, 2010, at the University of Phoenix Stadium in the Phoenix suburb of Glendale, Arizona.  This was the first WrestleMania since WrestleMania XI with a non-title match as a main event, the first WrestleMania to be held in the state of Arizona and the third to be held in an open-air venue, after WrestleMania IX and WrestleMania XXIV.

WrestleMania 32 | WrestleMania 32 was the thirty-second annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by WWE.  It took place on April 3, 2016, at the AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas.

WrestleMania XXV | WrestleMania XXV (also marketed as "The 25th Anniversary of WrestleMania") was the twenty-fifth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).  It was presented by the National Guard and took place on April 5, 2009, at Reliant Stadium in Houston, Texas.  The event featured talent from all three WWE brands: Raw, SmackDown, and ECW.  It was the second WrestleMania to be held in Houston, with the first being WrestleMania X-Seven at the Reliant Astrodome, which was held in 2001.

WrestleMania 33 | WrestleMania 33 was the thirty-third annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  In addition to standard PPV outlets, it was broadcast simultaneously on the WWE Network.  It took place on April 2, 2017 at Camping World Stadium in Orlando, Florida.  WrestleMania 33 was a joint-promotion event for the Raw and SmackDown brands, the first since WrestleMania XXVII in 2011 as the brand split ended that year, but was reintroduced in July 2016.
response.summary_2: WrestleMania 29 was held at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
